text.tspan: // Calculate actual block size with boundary check

div.p: Matrix: 1000×1000, block_size = 32 Full blocks: floor(1000/32) = 31 Remainder: 1000 - (31 × 32) = 8 Block grid: 32 × 32 blocks total

Edge blocks have lower efficiency: • Smaller working set → less cache reuse • Same loop overhead → higher overhead ratio • 8×8 corner: only 512 ops vs 32,768 for full block Impact is minimal for large matrices: • 961 full blocks vs 63 edge blocks • Edge blocks: ~6% of total computation

The min() boundary calculation ensures: ✓ No out-of-bounds array access ✓ All matrix elements processed exactly once ✓ Result matches naive implementation Key insight: The ternary operator handles both aligned and non-aligned cases uniformly.
